Container.Name: A1111-WebUI-Aegisnir
Container.Description:
AUTOMATIC1111 Stable Diffusion WebUI for Unraid.
DATA DIRECTORY WARNING: The path used with --data-dir grows very large very quickly because it can hold models, outputs, extensions, caches, and other working data. I recommend storing it outside of appdata when possible. You can still place it inside appdata if you want the convenience, but it may consume space very quickly and can fill the location you use for Docker-related storage.
The default host path is set to /mnt/user/ai/data/ so the large working set lives outside appdata by default.
Container.Overview: This template passes Automatic1111 data storage through --data-dir. - Models, outputs, extensions, caches, and other working data are stored in the mapped data share instead of the container filesystem. - This allows you to preserve your models and outputs across container updates and easily manage them from the host. - The data directory can grow very large very quickly, so I recommend storing it outside of appdata when possible. Appdata works if you prefer it, but be aware of how quickly it can fill your Docker storage area. - If startup fails with a Torch GPU check error, you can temporarily add --skip-torch-cuda-test to COMMANDLINE_ARGS for troubleshooting. I do not recommend using that as a permanent default because it can mask real GPU passthrough issues.
Container.Config: Host path for Automatic1111 --data-dir. Default is /mnt/user/ai/data/ so large models, outputs, caches, and extensions live outside appdata. Appdata works if you prefer it, but it may fill your Docker storage area faster than expected.

Extra arguments passed to launch.py. Includes --data-dir /data by default so models, outputs, caches, and extensions live in the mapped data share instead of the container filesystem. If you hit a Torch GPU check error during troubleshooting, you can temporarily append --skip-torch-cuda-test.
